commit.message:
fix: Auto-reinitialize client after failed reconnect + observe fire-and-forget DB tasks (#333)

## Problem

When a session's connection to the copilot CLI is lost and the reconnect
path in `SendPromptAsync` also fails, `_client` is set to `null` and
`IsInitialized` to `false`. Subsequent prompt attempts hit
`GetClientForGroup` which immediately throws **"Service not
initialized"** — before the client-recreation logic can run. This leaves
**ALL sessions permanently dead** until the user manually reconnects via
Settings.

**Crash log evidence:**
```
[ERROR] 'MEAI Hack' reconnect+retry failed, clearing IsProcessing
```
```
[TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException]
StreamJsonRpc.ConnectionLostException: The JSON-RPC connection with the remote party was lost
```

## Root Cause

In the reconnect path of `SendPromptAsync`,
`GetClientForGroup(sessionGroupId)` is called before the client
re-creation logic. When `_client == null` (from a previous failure), it
throws immediately, skipping the reconnection code entirely.

## Fix

### 1. Lazy client re-initialization (CopilotService.cs)
- Restart the persistent server if needed
- Create and start a new client
- Restore `IsInitialized = true`

This lets sessions self-heal on the next prompt attempt instead of being
permanently dead.

### 2. SafeFireAndForget for DB tasks (CopilotService.Utilities.cs + 16
call sites)
Replaced all `_ = _chatDb.AddMessageAsync(...)` and `_ =
_chatDb.UpdateReasoningContentAsync(...)` fire-and-forget calls with
`SafeFireAndForget(...)` — a static helper that observes the task via
`ContinueWith(OnlyOnFaulted)`. While `AddMessageAsync` has internal
`catch(Exception)`, this prevents `UnobservedTaskException` if the catch
is ever narrowed (historical regression pattern).

## Tests Added

- **SafeFireAndForget behavior** — completed, faulted, async-faulted,
and canceled tasks
- **Lazy re-init structural guards** — verify the guard exists before
`GetClientForGroup`, attempts server restart, and skips codespace
sessions
- **ChatDatabase broad-catch guard** — verify `AddMessageAsync` uses
`catch (Exception`
- **All ChatDb calls use SafeFireAndForget** — no bare `_ = _chatDb.`
patterns remain
- **Recovery scenario** — persistent failure → demo recovery → sessions
work

All 2305 tests pass. Mac Catalyst build succeeds.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Serialize gate step builds through XcodeBuildQueue

Gate steps (Build Check in Full Implementation template) were executing
xcodebuild directly via runShellCommand() without going through the
global XcodeBuildQueue actor. When multiple parallel worktrees hit their
Build Check gate simultaneously, concurrent xcodebuild processes would
collide and crash the app.

Now gate/deterministic shell steps detect build commands using
XcodeBuildQueue.isBuildCommand() and route them through
XcodeBuildQueue.shared.withSlot, matching the existing serialization
in TerminalToolsHandler for terminal.run builds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: preload only last 10 sprints instead of all 225

Previously the issue cache preloaded all sprints from 1 to maxSprint
(225+), firing 225 parallel gh API calls that hit rate limiting.

Now:
- Only preloads sprints that have actual GitHub milestones
- Limits to the 10 most recent sprints
- Loads sequentially (not parallel) to avoid rate limiting
- Background refresh only refreshes cached sprints, not all
- Older sprints load on-demand when navigated to

fix: SAS token expiry must not exceed delegation key expiry; upload blob in force-location path

Two bugs causing missing image placeholders:

1. blobSas.ts: expiresOn for the SAS token was computed before fetching the
   (potentially cached) delegation key. Azure Storage requires the SAS expiry
   to be <= the delegation key's signedExpiresOn. With a 7-day cached key, any
   SAS generated after the initial cache prime would have an expiry slightly
   beyond the key's validity, causing Azure to return 403 on every image load.
   Fix: fetch the delegation key first, then cap expiresOn at
   min(now + TTL, delegationKey.signedExpiresOn).

2. importHandler.ts (force-location path): imageUrl was set directly from
   message.attachments.first()?.url — a Discord CDN URL that expires in ~24h.
   uploadImageToBlob was never called. Fix: call uploadImageToBlob the same way
   pinProcessor does, falling back to existingPin.imageUrl if no attachment.

commit.message:
Precompute unassigned primary count and index set in ShardSummary (#2287)

`shards.unassigned.primary.present` rebuilds `new
Set(unassignedPrimaries.map(s => s.index).filter(Boolean))` on every
evaluation, even though the summary is already computed in a single
pass. This replaces the intermediate `CatShardRecord[]` with precomputed
aggregates.

- **`ShardSummary` interface**: `unassignedPrimaries: CatShardRecord[]`
→ `unassignedPrimaryCount: number` + `unassignedPrimaryIndices:
Set(string)`
- **`getShardSummary` loop**: increments count and adds to Set inline
instead of collecting records
- **`evaluate`**: consumes precomputed values directly, no per-call
allocation

```diff
-  unassignedPrimaries: CatShardRecord[];
+  unassignedPrimaryCount: number;
+  unassignedPrimaryIndices: Set(string);
```

Shard check median drops from 0.40ms → 0.03ms on the 50k-shard
benchmark. All 109 existing health check tests pass unchanged.

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#2280

repository.name: openmrs-module-legacyui
repository.description: The legacy user interface for OpenMRS Platform 2.x is chiefly comprised of administrative functions and the patient dashboard. Apparently, a new and more contemporary UI has been introduced via a UI framework and the legacy UI is kept around for administrative functions that are not yet implemented in the new UI. To retire the Legacy UI as planned, it is required to move the implementations and modules that still rely on it in order to maintain backwards compatibility. The main idea behind this project is to move legacy UI functions into an OpenMRS module that these implementations can install until they are able to migrate away from it, since most of the implementations of OpenMRS around the world are running OpenMRS 1.9.

commit.message:
Fix override optionality: derive parameter access from method parameter (#3832)

When @@override changes a parameter from required to optional, the HTTP
parameter's optional flag may not reflect the change. This caused the
modular emitter to generate positional references for parameters that
had moved into the options bag.

The fix introduces getEffectiveOptional() which resolves the method
parameter via methodParameterSegments and uses its optional flag,
following the same pattern getPathParameters() already uses correctly.
Client-level parameters (onClient) are excluded and continue to use
the HTTP parameter's own optional flag.

Updated isOptional(), isRequired(), and buildHeaderParameter() to use
getEffectiveOptional(). Added regression scenario for required-to-optional
override changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
